rule.instruction: Tissue paper cut into 1½-inch (4 cm) squares and inserted into holes in the gray pegboard walls. All holes in the walls are filled randomly.

designs.title: crisp mosaic
designs.intent: every hole filled, random direction+tone — crisp tonal mosaic (Klee #11 structural ground, random individual fills).

designs.title: crisp, high contrast
designs.intent: wide spacing range -> strong dark/light tonal scatter (Klee #9 weight).

designs.title: crisp, even tone
designs.intent: narrow spacing range -> calm, near-uniform field (Klee #7).

designs.title: crisp, sparse holes
designs.intent: bigger gaps between holes — the pegboard reads (Klee #7 breathing).

designs.title: soft mosaic
designs.intent: gentle hand-drawn fills — soft tissue feel (Klee #3).

designs.title: soft, even tone
designs.intent: soft and even — a quiet woven wall (Klee #3, #7).

designs.title: big tiles, crisp
designs.intent: fewer, larger tissue squares — bolder tonal blocks (Klee #9).

designs.title: fine grid, crisp
designs.intent: many small holes — a fine tonal pixel-field (Klee #11).

designs.title: fine grid, contrast
designs.intent: fine grid, high contrast — a shimmering mosaic (Klee #9).

designs.title: fine grid, even soft
designs.intent: fine, even, soft — the most textile-like (Klee #3, #7).
